steps.title: Distribute and simplify
steps.content:
Distribute $2a^2$ to each term in $(a + 3)$:

$$2a^2 \times (a + 3) = 2a^2 \cdot a + 2a^2 \cdot 3$$

Multiply each term:
  • $2a^2 \cdot a = 2a^3$ (when multiplying powers with the same base, add exponents: $a^2 \cdot a = a^{2+1} = a^3$)
  • $2a^2 \cdot 3 = 6a^2$

Combined: $2a^3 + 6a^2$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
